title: Make gathering feel satisfying (juice + feedback)
description: In the directly-interactive scene src/game/WorldScene.ts the player presses Space to harvest a tree/bush/rock/farm. Add real feedback: a small particle pop and a floating '+1 wood/food/stone' at the node, a brief shake/scale on the node, a soft synthesized sfx, and a short cooldown so spamming reads well. Node depletion should fade clearly. Player-facing only; do not touch src/sim.
verify: tsc + npm run build pass; gathering shows particles + floating gain text + sfx and depletion reads clearly (describe in AGENT_REPORT); src/sim untouched (sim still reaches the Information Age headless); no weakened tests.

title: Make building placement tactile
description: Improve the build flow in WorldScene: a dust/impact burst and sfx when a structure is placed, a clearer ghost (green=affordable, red + a reason flash when not), and a brief 'raising' tween as the building appears. Keep the grid-snapped placement and resource costs. Player-facing only.
verify: tsc + npm run build pass; placing/cancelling a building has clear visual+audio feedback and the unaffordable case is obvious; src/sim untouched; no weakened tests.

title: Tune chieftain movement & camera feel
description: Refine the player control in WorldScene: a touch of acceleration/deceleration, a small camera lead/deadzone so the view isn't dead-centered, a footstep cadence (subtle bob or dust), and retune PLAYER_SPEED so WASD and click-to-move both feel responsive and weighty. Keep the obstacle steering.
verify: tsc + npm run build pass; movement/camera feel improved (describe the tuning in AGENT_REPORT); collision/steering still works; src/sim untouched; no weakened tests.

title: Living day/night cycle in the interactive world
description: Add a running day/night clock to WorldScene (a render-side timer, independent of the paused sim): a smooth ambient tint cycle (dawn/day/dusk/night), campfires/huts glowing at night, and a small time-of-day indicator in the HUD. Pure render/UI; do not start the sim economy here.
verify: tsc + npm run build pass; the world visibly cycles day->night with a HUD indicator; src/sim untouched; no Phaser/DOM added to src/sim; no weakened tests.

title: Give villagers visible daily activities
description: Make the wandering tribe feel purposeful in WorldScene: some villagers walk to nearby trees/bushes and play a gather motion, some tend a placed farm, some gather around a campfire at night. Lightweight, render-side behaviour keyed off nearby gatherables/buildings; reuse the existing walk animation.
verify: tsc + npm run build pass; villagers show varied ambient activities tied to the world (describe in AGENT_REPORT); performant; src/sim untouched; no weakened tests.

title: First-run tutorial for the interactive mode
description: Add a dismissible, staged tutorial overlay in WorldScene that teaches the core loop on a fresh run only: move (WASD/click) -> gather (Space near a tree) -> open the build bar and place a hut -> talk to a villager marked ! to take a quest. Each step advances when the player does it; a Skip button. Persist 'tutorial seen' so it doesn't repeat.
verify: tsc + npm run build pass; the tutorial guides a new player through move/gather/build/quest and can be skipped, and does not reappear once completed; src/sim untouched; no weakened tests.

title: Quest log panel + more quest types
description: Build on the WorldScene quest system (givers marked !, turn-in marked ?). Add a toggleable quest-log panel listing all active quests with giver name + progress (the single banner only shows one). Add 2-3 new quest kinds beyond gather/build: 'explore region X', 'talk to N villagers', 'harvest from a farm you built'. Keep accept/turn-in via the giver.
verify: tsc + npm run build pass; multiple quests track at once in a log with names+progress and the new quest kinds complete correctly; src/sim untouched or pure; no weakened tests.

title: Branching dialogue with choices
description: Upgrade WorldScene villager dialogue from a single line to short branching conversations with 1-2 player choices (e.g. a villager asks for help -> Accept/Decline; lore/backstory branches). Reuse the sim's existing dialogue/naming/notable data for content. Choices can grant a small quest or flavor, not new sim systems.
verify: tsc + npm run build pass; talking to a villager shows a multi-line branching dialogue with selectable choices that have effects; src/sim untouched or pure; no weakened tests.

title: Surface tech/research into the interactive world
description: The sim already has a full tech tree (src/sim/knowledge.ts) but the interactive WorldScene doesn't expose it. Add an in-world way to direct research: e.g. talk to an elder or interact with a totem to open a compact tech panel (reuse data from knowledge.ts / the existing techgraph), pick the next tech, and reflect unlocked tech in the world (new buildables/abilities). Make an existing dormant system PLAYABLE; do not invent a new sim system.
verify: tsc + npm run build pass; the player can view and choose research from within WorldScene and see an effect; reads the existing knowledge/tech data; src/sim stays pure; sim still reaches the Information Age headless; no weakened tests.

title: Surface leaders & notable villagers in-world
description: The sim models leaders/chieftains and notable individuals (src/sim/leadership.ts, naming.ts) but the player never sees them. In WorldScene, mark the current leader and notable villagers (a crown/icon), let the player click them for an inspect card (name, role, trait bonus), and show the leader's tribe-wide bonus in the HUD. Surface the dormant system; no new sim system.
verify: tsc + npm run build pass; the leader and notable villagers are identifiable and inspectable in-world with their real traits/bonuses; reads existing sim data; src/sim untouched; no weakened tests.

title: Surface culture/belief as an in-world ritual
description: The sim has a culture/belief track (src/sim/culture.ts) with no interactive surface. Add an in-world interaction (e.g. a ritual at the campfire, or building a totem) that accrues culture, with a small HUD indicator and a visible effect when a culture milestone is reached. Make the existing system playable; do not add a new sim system.
verify: tsc + npm run build pass; a player action accrues culture with clear in-world/HUD feedback tied to the existing culture data; src/sim stays pure; sim still reaches the Information Age; no weakened tests.

title: Bring rival tribes into the explorable world
description: The sim has rival/neighbour tribes (src/sim/rivals.ts, diplomacy/trade/raids) only shown on the old unmounted map. In WorldScene, place a reachable neighbour camp the player can walk to, with their disposition shown, and surface one diplomacy interaction (trade or a gift) as an in-world action with a real effect. Surface the dormant system; no new sim system.
verify: tsc + npm run build pass; a rival camp exists in the world and the player can perform a diplomacy interaction with a real outcome from the existing rivals data; src/sim untouched; no weakened tests.

title: Interactive raid / camp defense
description: Add an interactive defense event in WorldScene: a hostile rival party approaches the camp; the outcome depends on the chieftain, nearby villagers, defensive buildings, and defensive tech (reuse src/sim/raids.ts logic for resolution). Keep it bounded: a clear threat indicator, a short defend interaction (rally villagers / a brief skirmish), and a resolved result with consequences.
verify: tsc + npm run build pass; a raid event plays out interactively with an outcome driven by the existing raid/defense math; src/sim stays pure; sim still reaches the Information Age; no weakened tests.

title: Re-activate survival: gathering & housing drive the tribe
description: Carefully wire the sim's survival loop into WorldScene so the world has stakes: run a SLOW sim/economy tick while playing, where the food the player gathers feeds the tribe, housing (huts) caps/grows population, and scarcity/season matters. Bounded scope: enable the tick + food/housing consequences + a clear HUD readout (food trend, pop, housing) — not the full management UI. Pause the tick during tutorial/dialog as needed.
verify: tsc + npm run build pass; npm run sim still reaches the Information Age in range; the interactive tribe now grows/struggles based on the player's gathering and housing with a clear HUD readout; src/sim stays pure (no Phaser/DOM); no weakened tests; any sim change is covered by a test.

title: Diegetic audio for the interactive world
description: Wire sound into WorldScene (reuse/extend src/ui/audio.ts, synthesized, off until a user gesture): footstep, gather, build, quest-accept/complete, and raid cues, plus a gentle ambient bed that shifts with biome and time-of-day. A mute toggle. No external audio assets.
verify: tsc + npm run build pass; interactive actions trigger appropriate sfx and an ambient bed plays, all behind a mute toggle and only after a user gesture; src/sim untouched; no weakened tests.

title: Accessibility & controls for the interactive scene
description: Make WorldScene playable and legible for everyone: full keyboard-only play (move, gather, open build bar, place, talk, all without a mouse), a toggleable controls/help overlay (key: ? or H), colourblind-safe quest/disposition markers (shape + colour), and larger/clearer interaction hit targets and prompts.
verify: tsc + npm run build pass; the interactive game is fully playable with keyboard only and a controls overlay + colourblind-safe markers exist; src/sim untouched; no weakened tests.

title: Keep the interactive scene smooth at scale
description: Profile WorldScene as the camp grows (many villagers, buildings, gatherables, particles) and keep it smooth: cull/disable off-camera entities and per-frame work, cap concurrent particles, clean up depleted nodes and finished tweens, and avoid per-frame allocations in the update loop. Behaviour-preserving.
verify: tsc + npm run build pass; AGENT_REPORT shows a concrete reduction in per-frame work/allocations or off-screen culling with no behaviour change; src/sim untouched; no weakened tests.

title: Save & load the interactive world
description: Persist the interactive play session: player position, placed buildings, gathered resources, quest state, and fog-of-war reveal — folded into the existing versioned save (src/sim save-versioning) with a safe default for older saves. A Save/Load control in WorldScene.
verify: tsc + npm run build pass; a round-trip restores player position, buildings, resources, quests and fog; a test covers the new persisted state + an older-save default; npm run sim still reaches the Information Age; src/sim stays pure; no weakened tests.
